edges.description: Abnormal Cav1.4 channel function impairs calcium-dependent signaling at rod and cone photoreceptor synapses.
Photoreceptor-to-bipolar synaptic dysfunction produces reduced photopic and scotopic retinal responses on electroretinography.

edges.description: Abnormal retinal signal transmission manifests clinically as poor acuity, nystagmus, myopia, color-vision defects, and impaired dark adaptation.
